title: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY documented in .env.example
evidence: .env.example line 481: # STREAM_SIGNING_KEY= with generation instructions; line 485: # STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS=
expected: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY and STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS documented in .env.example
actual: Both keys documented with rotation instructions
recommendation: No action required - documentation is complete

title: streams.ts missing reconnectOnError that client.ts implements
evidence: client.ts line 241-243: reconnectOnError handler reconnects on READONLY errors (Redis failover); streams.ts has no reconnectOnError entry in its options block (confirmed by grep)
expected: Both Redis clients have identical reconnectOnError behavior for failover resilience
actual: streams.ts lacks reconnectOnError - during a Redis primary failover, stream messages could be lost if the new primary briefly emits READONLY errors
recommendation: Add reconnectOnError: (err) => err.message?.includes(READONLY) ?? false to the ioredis options in RedisStreamsClient constructor (shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts around line 468)

title: FEATURE_MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN enabled without FEATURE_MEV_SHARE cross-dependency check
evidence: feature-flags.ts: useMevShareBackrun (line 339) and useMevShare (line 353) are independent flags; validateFeatureFlags() at lines 769-773 logs when MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN is enabled but does NOT check if FEATURE_MEV_SHARE is also enabled. The MEV-Share backrun strategy sends bundles via MEV-Share protocol which typically requires the MEV_SHARE rebate mode to be configured
expected: validateFeatureFlags() warns when FEATURE_MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN=true but FEATURE_MEV_SHARE=false
actual: No cross-dependency validation between FEATURE_MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN and FEATURE_MEV_SHARE
recommendation: Add warning in validateFeatureFlags() when useMevShareBackrun is true but useMevShare is false, similar to the existing SIGNAL_CACHE_READ -> ML_SIGNAL_SCORING check

title: DLQ auto-recovery catch block swallows replay errors without logging
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/consumers/dlq-consumer.ts:361 - catch { failed++ } with no log call, silently increments failure counter
expected: Catch block should log the actual error reason before incrementing failed counter
actual: Error details are lost; only a count increment happens. The broader log at line 367 only reports totals, not per-message failure reasons
recommendation: Add logger.warn/error inside catch block with messageId and error details to enable diagnosis of repeated DLQ replay failures

title: PancakeSwap V3 flash-loan provider silently skips fee tier failures
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/strategies/flash-loan-providers/pancakeswap-v3.provider.ts:289 - catch { continue } with no logging (also line 240 has comment but 289 does not)
expected: Fee tier query failures should be logged at debug level to diagnose pool-not-found vs RPC errors
actual: Line 289 catch block has no comment and no log — completely silent failure that skips fee tier selection silently
recommendation: Add logger.debug call in the bare continue catch at line 289 to distinguish pool-not-found (expected) from RPC errors (unexpected)

title: All 23 FEATURE_* flags documented in .env.example
evidence: Cross-checked all flags in FEATURE_FLAGS object against .env.example: FEATURE_BATCHED_QUOTER, FEATURE_FLASH_LOAN_AGGREGATOR, FEATURE_COMMIT_REVEAL, FEATURE_COMMIT_REVEAL_REDIS, FEATURE_DEST_CHAIN_FLASH_LOAN, FEATURE_MOMENTUM_TRACKING, FEATURE_ML_SIGNAL_SCORING, FEATURE_SIGNAL_CACHE_READ, FEATURE_LIQUIDITY_DEPTH_SIZING, FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES, FEATURE_ORDERFLOW_PIPELINE, FEATURE_KMS_SIGNING, FEATURE_FAST_LANE, FEATURE_BACKRUN_STRATEGY, FEATURE_UNISWAPX_FILLER, FEATURE_MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN, FEATURE_SOLANA_EXECUTION, FEATURE_STATISTICAL_ARB, FEATURE_MEV_SHARE, FEATURE_ADAPTIVE_RISK_SCORING, FEATURE_TIMEBOOST, FEATURE_FLASHBOTS_PROTECT_L2, FEATURE_COW_BACKRUN - all present in .env.example
expected: All flags documented
actual: All 23 flags documented with defaults and descriptions
recommendation: No action required

title: Total empty/near-empty catch block count in production source files
evidence: Across services/ and shared/ source files (excluding tests): ~35 catch{} blocks found. Of these: 6 have comments explaining intentional swallowing (whale-analyzer, cross-chain strategy, detector, chain-instance, snapshot-manager, coordinator/stream-consumer-manager). ~29 have log calls or explicit return values as fallback. 2 are borderline (SA-100, SA-101 above).
expected: All catch blocks should either log the error or have an explanatory comment
actual: Most catch blocks are properly commented. 2 production catch blocks lack adequate error observability.
recommendation: No systemic issue. Address SA-100 and SA-101 individually.

title: All risk env vars documented in .env.example
evidence: .env.example lines 940-1005: documents RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL (line 954, noted as REQUIRED for production), RISK_KELLY_MULTIPLIER, RISK_MAX_SINGLE_TRADE, RISK_MIN_EV_THRESHOLD, RISK_MIN_WIN_PROBABILITY, RISK_MAX_LOSS_PER_TRADE and all other risk vars
expected: All RISK_* vars documented with production requirements noted
actual: Complete documentation present including the defaultWinProbability >= minWinProbability cross-validation warning
recommendation: No action required

title: Stream serialization numeric fields use .toString() with parseFloat() on deserialization
evidence: services/coordinator/src/utils/stream-serialization.ts:49-62 converts profitPercentage, confidence, expectedProfit, estimatedProfit via .toString(). services/execution-engine/src/consumers/validation.ts:327-337 correctly restores them with parseFloat() before returning.
expected: Numeric fields serialized as strings must be parsed back to numbers on the consumer side
actual: Correct round-trip: .toString() on serialize, parseFloat() on deserialize. No type fidelity loss.
recommendation: No action needed. Pattern is intentional and correctly implemented.

title: Risk config cross-validates defaultWinProbability >= minWinProbability
evidence: validateRiskConfig() at line 328-334: throws if probability.defaultWinProbability < ev.minWinProbability with clear error explaining new chains after restart would be rejected; validated at import time (line 342) with production throw behavior
expected: Cross-validation between defaultWinProbability and minWinProbability
actual: Implemented correctly - throws on violation
recommendation: No action required

title: parseStreamResult parses all values via JSON.parse — numbers stored as strings will become strings unless valid JSON
evidence: shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:1395-1399: data[key] = JSON.parse(value) with catch { data[key] = value }. Redis stores all fields as strings. JSON.parse('0.5') = 0.5 (number, correct). But Redis stores stream fields as raw strings, so profitPercentage='0.5' is parsed as number 0.5 by JSON.parse. This works correctly.
expected: Numeric fields stored as strings in Redis should be restored to number type
actual: JSON.parse('0.5') successfully returns 0.5 as a number, so profitPercentage arrives at validation.ts as a number type already. The parseFloat fallback in validation.ts is a defensive guard.
recommendation: Behavior is correct. The defensive parseFloat in validation.ts is appropriate belt-and-suspenders protection.

title: parseFloat(EXECUTION_STREAM_BACKPRESSURE_RATIO) without NaN guard - NaN silently disables backpressure
evidence: coordinator.ts line 422: parseFloat(process.env.EXECUTION_STREAM_BACKPRESSURE_RATIO ?? 0.95) - ?? only guards null/undefined not malformed strings; line 428: this.executionStreamBackpressureRatio = this.config.executionStreamBackpressureRatio ?? 0.95 - ?? also does NOT replace NaN; line 2038: if (this.cachedExecutionStreamDepthRatio > this.executionStreamBackpressureRatio) - NaN comparison is always false, silently disabling backpressure protection
expected: NaN-safe parse with fallback to 0.95
actual: Malformed env var (e.g., EXECUTION_STREAM_BACKPRESSURE_RATIO=abc) silently disables execution stream backpressure; execution queue can grow unbounded
recommendation: Replace with: const raw = parseFloat(process.env.EXECUTION_STREAM_BACKPRESSURE_RATIO ?? 0.95); and guard: Number.isNaN(raw) ? 0.95 : raw; or use safeParseFloat from shared utils

title: Unsafe 'as unknown as' casts in DLQ consumer lack runtime validation
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/consumers/dlq-consumer.ts:212,321,437 - message.data cast directly to DlqMessage via 'as unknown as DlqMessage' with no schema validation
expected: Stream messages cast to typed structs should have runtime field validation
actual: DlqMessage fields are accessed directly after cast; a malformed message would pass type checking but cause runtime errors when fields are accessed. No zod/validator guard is present.
recommendation: Add a isDlqMessage() type guard function that validates required fields (opportunityId, errorCode, timestamp) before the cast, or wrap access in try/catch with specific error logging for corrupt DLQ messages

title: parseFloat(BALANCE_MONITOR_LOW_THRESHOLD_ETH) without NaN guard - NaN disables low-balance alerts
evidence: engine.ts line 673: lowBalanceThresholdEth: parseFloat(process.env.BALANCE_MONITOR_LOW_THRESHOLD_ETH ?? 0.01) - no NaN check before or after; if BALANCE_MONITOR_LOW_THRESHOLD_ETH=abc then threshold becomes NaN; any balance comparison against NaN returns false, silently disabling low-balance warnings during live trading
expected: NaN-safe parse with fallback to 0.01
actual: Malformed BALANCE_MONITOR_LOW_THRESHOLD_ETH silently disables the low-balance protection circuit, risking trade failures from insufficient gas funds going undetected
recommendation: Use: Number.isNaN(parsed) ? 0.01 : parsed or call parseEnvIntSafe/safeParseFloat equivalent

title: Redis key prefix inventory: 9 unique prefixes identified across services
evidence: Prefixes found: 'lock:execution:' (execution-engine/engine.ts:443), 'bridge:recovery:' (execution-engine/types.ts:1165), 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:' (bridge-recovery-manager.ts:483), 'commit-reveal:' (commit-reveal.service.ts), 'price:' (unified-detector cache + price-oracle.ts), 'coordinator:leader:lock' (coordinator integration tests), 'risk:probabilities:' (shared/config/risk-config.ts:226), 'stream:' (stream names in redis-streams.ts), A/B metrics prefix (configurable via redisKeyPrefix in ab-testing/types.ts)
expected: No prefix collisions or undocumented prefixes
actual: 9 prefixes identified. No documented registry file found in codebase.
recommendation: Create a docs/redis-key-registry.md or add to CONFIGURATION.md to document all Redis key prefixes, owners, TTL policies, and namespace isolation strategy

title: parseInt(HEALTH_CHECK_PORT) without NaN guard in cross-chain-detector and execution-engine
evidence: cross-chain-detector/src/index.ts line 23: parseInt(process.env.HEALTH_CHECK_PORT || process.env.CROSS_CHAIN_DETECTOR_PORT || 3006, 10) - uses || which protects against empty string but not against non-numeric values like 3006abc; execution-engine/src/index.ts line 46: parseInt(process.env.HEALTH_CHECK_PORT || process.env.EXECUTION_ENGINE_PORT || 3005, 10) - same pattern; if HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=notaport parseInt returns NaN, passed to server.listen(NaN) which Node.js treats as port 0 (random OS-assigned port), health endpoint becomes unreachable
expected: NaN-safe port parse - use parsePort() helper as unified-detector does (constants.ts line 61)
actual: Malformed HEALTH_CHECK_PORT causes health server to bind to random port, making health checks and monitoring unreliable
recommendation: Use parsePort() from shared utils (as unified-detector/src/index.ts line 61 does) or add Number.isNaN check with fallback

title: 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:' prefix is a substring collision with 'bridge:recovery:' — SCAN patterns must be careful
evidence: bridge-recovery-manager.ts:483 creates keys 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:{original_key}'. The periodic scanner (src/services/bridge-recovery-manager.ts) scans for 'bridge:recovery:*' which would also match 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:*' keys
expected: Corrupt bridge keys should be in a separate namespace not matched by the main recovery scan pattern
actual: If the periodic scanner uses 'bridge:recovery:*' it will find corrupt keys too. These keys contain the original key path as part of their key name (e.g. 'bridge:recovery:corrupt:bridge:recovery:bad-id'), which could cause double-prefix corruption on re-scan
recommendation: Change corrupt key prefix to 'bridge:corrupt:' or 'dlq:bridge:' to prevent SCAN pattern collision with the main recovery scanner

title: Simulation config parseFloat/parseInt in execution-engine index.ts without NaN guards
evidence: index.ts lines 65-69: successRate=parseFloat(EXECUTION_SIMULATION_SUCCESS_RATE||0.85), executionLatencyMs=parseInt(...), gasUsed=parseInt(...), gasCostMultiplier=parseFloat(...), profitVariance=parseFloat(...) - all use || default which protects NaN case for parseFloat but only when the var is empty/undefined; non-numeric values like abc would still produce NaN since || checks falsy. In contrast, engine.ts lines 362-373 shows the correct pattern with explicit Number.isNaN validation for AB testing config
expected: All simulation config parses use NaN guard (Number.isNaN check after parse)
actual: 5 simulation config values may propagate NaN if env vars contain non-numeric text; simulation mode runs in test environments where malformed config can cause silent failures
recommendation: Apply the same Number.isNaN validation pattern as engine.ts lines 368-377 to simulation config in index.ts

title: partition-solana service-config.ts uses raw parseFloat/parseInt without NaN guards
evidence: service-config.ts line 83: minProfitThreshold=parseFloat(MIN_PROFIT_THRESHOLD ?? String(DEFAULT_MIN_PROFIT_THRESHOLD)), line 86: maxTriangularDepth=parseInt(MAX_TRIANGULAR_DEPTH ?? 3, 10), line 87: opportunityExpiryMs=parseInt(OPPORTUNITY_EXPIRY_MS ?? 1000, 10), line 90: defaultTradeValueUsd=parseFloat(SOLANA_DEFAULT_TRADE_VALUE_USD ?? 1000) - none have NaN protection; healthCheckPort (line 153) correctly uses parsePort() helper but opportunity config does not
expected: All numeric env var parses use NaN guard or safe parse helpers
actual: minProfitThreshold NaN would cause all Solana opportunities to fail profit check; maxTriangularDepth NaN would cause arbitrage path depth to be unbound
recommendation: Use parsePort() helper pattern or add Number.isNaN checks with fallback values after each parse

title: ADR-022 (Hot-Path Memory): No spread operators in loops in hot-path files
evidence: price-matrix.ts: spread operators only in getConfig() and getStats() (line 507, 1065) — not in loops. simple-arbitrage-detector.ts: spread only in logger call and getStats() (lines 328,334,345) — not in loops. No spread-in-loop pattern found in hot-path files.
expected: No spread operators in tight loops per ADR-022
actual: PASS — spread operators exist only in stats/config accessor methods, not in detection or execution loops
recommendation: No action needed

title: Well-protected parseInt/parseFloat patterns confirmed in critical paths
evidence: engine.ts lines 395-407: MAX_CONCURRENT_EXECUTIONS and MAX_CONCURRENT_PER_CHAIN use explicit Number.isNaN guards; gas-price-optimizer.ts line 138-150: validateGasPrice() wraps all parseFloat calls with Number.isNaN check; engine.ts lines 368-377: AB_TESTING config uses full NaN validation; coordinator/src/api/middleware/index.ts lines 62-63: uses || fallback (double protection - || handles both NaN and empty/null)
expected: Critical concurrency and gas price configs are NaN-safe
actual: Confirmed safe - these critical paths have proper guards
recommendation: No action required for these paths; apply similar patterns to SA-058, SA-059, SA-060, SA-061, SA-062

title: ADR-033 (Stale Price Window): maxPriceAgeMs default correctly = 30000ms in cross-chain detector
evidence: services/cross-chain-detector/src/detector.ts:1458 - 'const maxPriceAgeMs = this.config.maxPriceAgeMs ?? 30000'. types.ts:335 documents default as 30000 (30s). ADR-033 specifies 30000ms. Note: STALE_PRICE_THRESHOLD named constant not used — value is inlined as literal 30000 with ?? fallback.
expected: maxPriceAgeMs default = 30000ms per ADR-033
actual: PASS — correctly implemented as ?? 30000 fallback
recommendation: Consider extracting 30000 to a named constant STALE_PRICE_WINDOW_MS for clarity, but not a compliance violation

title: retryStrategy behavior is identical between client.ts and streams.ts
evidence: client.ts lines 223-236 and streams.ts lines 468-483: both use Math.min(Math.round(200 * Math.pow(2, Math.min(times-1, 18))), 60000) capped exponential backoff; both NEVER return null (no stop-reconnecting behavior); both log warn at attempt 1 and every 5th attempt; both log error after 30 attempts
expected: Identical capped exponential backoff with persistent reconnection
actual: Behavior matches exactly
recommendation: No action required

title: connectTimeout and maxRetriesPerRequest match between client.ts and streams.ts
evidence: client.ts: connectTimeout=5000 (line 239), maxRetriesPerRequest=3 (line 218), lazyConnect=true (line 219); streams.ts: connectTimeout=5000 (line 490), maxRetriesPerRequest=3 (line 485), lazyConnect=true (line 486) - all three parameters are identical
expected: Matching connectTimeout, maxRetriesPerRequest, and lazyConnect values
actual: All three parameters match exactly
recommendation: No action required; note SA-052 for the reconnectOnError divergence

title: ADR-002 (Redis Streams Only): Direct inter-service HTTP URLs found in CORS allowlist
evidence: services/coordinator/src/api/middleware/index.ts:84 - allowedOrigins includes 'http://localhost:3000' and 'http://localhost:3001' as CORS origins (not internal service calls). Test file services/coordinator/__tests__/unit/api/middleware.test.ts:218,248 validates this behavior.
expected: No direct inter-service HTTP calls (ADR-002 requires Redis Streams for service-to-service communication)
actual: These are CORS allowlist entries for browser dashboard access, NOT inter-service RPC calls. True inter-service communication uses Redis Streams. No ADR-002 violation found.
recommendation: PASS with clarification: CORS allowlist http://localhost entries are for local development dashboard, not ADR-002 inter-service calls. Confirm CORS config is removed or restricted to specific domains in production deployment.

title: All service port assignments match service-ports.json registry
evidence: Registry: coordinator=3000, partition-asia-fast=3001, partition-l2-turbo=3002, partition-high-value=3003, partition-solana=3004, execution-engine=3005, cross-chain-detector=3006, unified-detector=3007, mempool-detector=3008, monolith=3100; Verified against: coordinator/src/index.ts default 3000, partition-*/.*/index.ts defaults 3001-3004, execution-engine/src/index.ts default 3005, cross-chain-detector/src/index.ts default 3006, unified-detector/src/constants.ts DEFAULT_HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=3007, mempool-detector/src/types.ts DEFAULT_MEMPOOL_DETECTOR_PORT=3008, monolith/src/index.ts MONOLITH_HEALTH_PORT default 3100
expected: All service ports match registry
actual: All ports match exactly
recommendation: No action required

title: ADR-018 (Circuit Breaker): Default failureThreshold = 5 confirmed
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/types.ts:681 - DEFAULT_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CONFIG.failureThreshold = 5. services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:89 - CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD env var default '5'. services/coordinator/src/alerts/notifier.ts:47 - DEFAULT_CIRCUIT_CONFIG.failureThreshold = 5.
expected: Default failureThreshold = 5 per ADR-018
actual: PASS — all three circuit breaker implementations use default 5
recommendation: No action needed

title: ADR-005 (Hierarchical Cache): SharedArrayBuffer used in price-matrix.ts
evidence: shared/core/src/caching/price-matrix.ts:224,438-450 - SharedArrayBuffer is created and used as primary backing storage. Falls back to regular Float64Array only when SharedArrayBuffer is unavailable. Used for L1 price cache with Atomics for concurrent access.
expected: SharedArrayBuffer used for L1 price cache per ADR-005
actual: PASS — SharedArrayBuffer is the primary storage path. Fallback to ArrayBuffer only when unavailable (e.g., COOP/COEP headers not set).
recommendation: No action needed

title: Monolith assigns coordinator worker to port 3009 which is not in the service-ports.json registry
evidence: monolith/src/index.ts line 104: HEALTH_CHECK_PORT: 3009 for the coordinator worker inside the monolith process; comment says this avoids conflict with monolith health server on 3100; service-ports.json lists coordinator=3000 with no entry for 3009 - the registry is incomplete for monolith in-process port assignments
expected: All ports used by services are registered in service-ports.json
actual: Port 3009 used by coordinator within monolith is undocumented in the registry
recommendation: Add a monolith-internal section to service-ports.json documenting that coordinator within monolith uses 3009, or add 3009 as coordinator-in-monolith to avoid surprises during port conflict troubleshooting

title: Execution-engine Fly.io config uses port 8080 vs service-ports.json value of 3005
evidence: infrastructure/fly/execution-engine.toml: internal_port=8080, HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=8080. shared/constants/service-ports.json: execution-engine=3005. services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:46 reads HEALTH_CHECK_PORT env var, defaulting to 3005 if not set.
expected: Fly.io port matches service-ports.json canonical value
actual: Port intentionally overridden to 8080 via HEALTH_CHECK_PORT env var in Fly TOML. Comment in TOML acknowledges this: '# - Internal port 8080 (overrides code default of 3005 via HEALTH_CHECK_PORT env var)'. This creates documentation confusion.
recommendation: Add a note in shared/constants/service-ports.json or service-ports.json README that execution-engine uses 8080 in Fly.io deployment. Alternatively add a flyPort field to the JSON schema.

title: No port collisions detected between registered services
evidence: All 10 service ports are unique: 3000, 3001, 3002, 3003, 3004, 3005, 3006, 3007, 3008, 3100; monolith in-process assignments 3001-3006 are worker subprocesses (not listening hosts), monolith health server is 3100; no two standalone services share a port
expected: No two services assigned the same port
actual: All service ports are unique; no collision detected
recommendation: No action required

title: monolith/src/index.ts uses plain parseInt for MONOLITH_HEALTH_PORT and SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS without NaN guards
evidence: index.ts line 63: const HEALTH_PORT = parseInt(process.env.MONOLITH_HEALTH_PORT ?? 3100, 10) - no NaN check; line 64: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS = parseInt(process.env.MONOLITH_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS ?? 30000, 10) - no NaN check; if MONOLITH_HEALTH_PORT=disabled then HEALTH_PORT=NaN causing server.listen(NaN) to bind to random port; if MONOLITH_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=abc then graceful drain is skipped (Math.max(0, NaN)=NaN, setTimeout(fn, NaN) fires immediately)
expected: NaN-safe parseInt with fallback for health port and shutdown timeout
actual: Malformed env vars can cause health server bind failure or immediate shutdown without drain period
recommendation: Apply Number.isNaN guard: const raw = parseInt(MONOLITH_HEALTH_PORT ?? 3100, 10); const HEALTH_PORT = Number.isNaN(raw) ? 3100 : raw;

title: All service Dockerfiles use node:22-alpine matching engines requirement
evidence: All 8 service Dockerfiles (coordinator, cross-chain-detector, execution-engine, partition-asia-fast, partition-high-value, partition-l2-turbo, partition-solana, unified-detector) use FROM node:22-alpine. Package.json engines field requires >=22.0.0.
expected: node:22-alpine in all Dockerfiles per CLAUDE.md guideline
actual: PASS — all Dockerfiles use node:22-alpine
recommendation: No action needed

title: Health check paths consistent: /api/health for coordinator, /health for all other services
evidence: infrastructure/fly/coordinator.toml: path='/api/health'. All other TOMLs (cross-chain-detector, execution-engine, partition-*): path='/health'. docker-compose.yml matches: coordinator uses '/api/health' (W2-L6 FIX comment), others use '/health'.
expected: Health check paths match between Fly.io TOMLs and Docker Compose
actual: PASS — paths are consistent across infrastructure configs
recommendation: No action needed

title: Execution-engine shutdown timeout hierarchy is correctly ordered
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:415,416,420: shutdownTimeoutMs = drainTimeoutMs(30000) + POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER_MS(15000) = 45000ms total. SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS default=30000ms. SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS in types.ts=5000ms (used for Redis/stream disconnect). connectTimeout for Redis=5000ms (streams.ts:490, client.ts:239). Hierarchy: total shutdown (45s) > drain (30s) > post-drain cleanup (15s) > redis-connect (5s).
expected: shutdown > drain > redis-connect
actual: PASS — 45000 > 30000 > 5000ms. Hierarchy is correct.
recommendation: No action needed

title: Cross-chain detector SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS (5000ms) may be too short relative to Redis connect timeout (5000ms)
evidence: services/cross-chain-detector/src/detector.ts:501 - SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000ms used for both streamsClient disconnect and Redis disconnect via disconnectWithTimeout(). shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:490 - connectTimeout=5000ms. If Redis connection is slow to close, shutdown may conflict with new connection attempts.
expected: shutdown disconnect timeout > redis connectTimeout for clean separation
actual: Both are 5000ms (equal). In practice disconnectWithTimeout() is closing an existing connection, not connecting, so latency profiles differ. Not a hard violation but worth monitoring.
recommendation: Consider increasing SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS to 8000ms in cross-chain-detector to provide buffer above the 5000ms redis connectTimeout, or document the intentional equality

title: Partition services use SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000ms from shared health-server.ts with HEALTH_SERVER_CLOSE_TIMEOUT_MS=1000ms for server close
evidence: shared/core/src/partition/health-server.ts:410 - SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000ms. Line 413: HEALTH_SERVER_CLOSE_TIMEOUT_MS=1000ms. Line 492: closeServerWithTimeout(healthServer, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000). Detector stop also uses SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000ms. Redis connectTimeout=5000ms.
expected: shutdown > server-close. Partition shutdown hierarchy: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS(5000) > HEALTH_SERVER_CLOSE_TIMEOUT_MS(1000)
actual: PASS — 5000 > 1000ms. Hierarchy is correct for partition services.
recommendation: No action needed

title: Hardcoded timeout values found in multiple services without env var override
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:416 - POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER_MS=15000 hardcoded. shared/core/src/partition/health-server.ts:410 - SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 hardcoded (no env var). shared/core/src/partition/health-server.ts:413 - HEALTH_SERVER_CLOSE_TIMEOUT_MS=1000 hardcoded. services/execution-engine/src/services/simulation/anvil-manager.ts:148 - DEFAULT_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 hardcoded.
expected: Shutdown timeouts configurable via environment variables per operational best practices
actual: 4 hardcoded timeout constants found. SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS IS configurable (env var). POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER_MS and partition SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS are not.
recommendation: Consider adding PARTITION_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS and POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER_MS env vars for operational flexibility, especially for high-latency cloud environments

title: HMAC signing is fail-closed in production
evidence: streams.ts lines 454-461: constructor throws Error when signingKey is null and NODE_ENV equals production; factory function at lines 1561-1569 resolves STREAM_SIGNING_KEY from env, trims whitespace, and warns if empty; production fail-closed path verified
expected: Missing HMAC key in production causes startup failure (fail-closed)
actual: Constructor throws: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY is required in production - verified correct
recommendation: No action required - production enforcement is correct

title: Core feature flag cross-dependencies are validated at startup
evidence: validateFeatureFlags(): line 653 checks useSignalCacheRead and useMLSignalScoring disabled (warns); line 573-598 checks useCommitRevealRedis with missing REDIS_URL (throws in production, warns in dev); line 744-757 checks useSolanaExecution with missing SOLANA_RPC_URL (warns)
expected: Cross-dependency violations are caught at startup
actual: Validated correctly with appropriate severity (production throw vs dev warn)
recommendation: No action required for core cross-dependencies; see SA-053 for MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN gap

title: Stream names: no hardcoded literals in production code
evidence: Grep for stream:[a-z-]+ in services/**/*.ts (excluding __tests__) returned only comment lines (coordinator.ts:326, opportunity-router.ts:98-100). All functional stream name arguments use RedisStreams.* constants from @arbitrage/types.
expected: All stream name arguments use RedisStreams constants
actual: Confirmed — no hardcoded stream name string literals in production code paths
recommendation: No action required. Continue enforcing via code review.

title: Consumer group names consistent with expected set
evidence: Found groups: coordinator-group (coordinator.ts:394), cross-chain-detector-group (detector.ts:331,344,351), execution-engine-group (opportunity.consumer.ts:168 and fast-lane.consumer.ts:102), mempool-detector-group (mempool-config.ts:77 via MEMPOOL_CONSUMER_GROUP env default), orderflow-pipeline (orderflow-pipeline-consumer.ts:85), failover-{serviceName} (cross-region-health.ts:750 dynamic).
expected: coordinator-group, cross-chain-detector-group, execution-engine-group, mempool-detector-group, orderflow-pipeline, failover-{serviceName}
actual: All 6 expected group patterns present. fast-lane consumer intentionally reuses execution-engine-group (P1 Fix CA-005).
recommendation: No drift detected.

title: All 24 streams covered by STREAM_MAX_LENGTHS; no raw xadd bypasses
evidence: RedisStreamsClient.STREAM_MAX_LENGTHS in shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:402-430 defines 24 entries matching all 24 RedisStreams constants. StreamBatcher uses xaddWithLimit (line 290). DLQ routing uses xaddWithLimit (line 876). opportunity-router uses xaddWithLimit. stream-consumer-manager DLQ write uses xaddWithLimit (line 469). No direct redis.xadd() bypasses found in services/.
expected: All 24 streams have MAXLEN; no raw xadd bypasses
actual: Full coverage confirmed.
recommendation: No action required.

title: XACK present in all consumer-group files
evidence: Files with xReadGroup: shared/core/src/redis/stream-consumer.ts (xack at lines 262, 371), shared/core/src/analytics/orderflow-pipeline-consumer.ts (xack at line 346), shared/core/src/monitoring/cross-region-health.ts (xack at line 808). Services use StreamConsumer class which handles ACK internally. DLQ consumer uses xread (no group) so no ACK required.
expected: All consumer-group consumers also ACK messages
actual: All files either directly xack or delegate to StreamConsumer class. No missing ACK found.
recommendation: No action required.

title: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY and STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS not documented in .env.example
evidence: process.env.STREAM_SIGNING_KEY used in shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts — enforced non-null in production, throws Error on startup if missing. STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS also used for key rotation (OP-17). Both absent from .env.example.
expected: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY and STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS documented in .env.example
actual: Both absent — production deployment will fail to start if operator does not know to set STREAM_SIGNING_KEY.
recommendation: Add STREAM_SIGNING_KEY and STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS to .env.example with comments explaining HMAC signing requirement.

title: JWT_SECRET not documented in .env.example
evidence: process.env.JWT_SECRET used in shared/security/src/auth.ts:90,94 — throws if not set. JWT_EXPIRES_IN also used at line 95. Neither var appears in .env.example.
expected: JWT_SECRET documented in .env.example
actual: JWT_SECRET absent from .env.example. Auth middleware will throw on initialization if not set.
recommendation: Add JWT_SECRET and JWT_EXPIRES_IN to .env.example with security guidance.

title: Chain private key env vars not documented in .env.example
evidence: WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY (shared/config), ETHEREUM_PRIVATE_KEY, BSC_PRIVATE_KEY, ARBITRUM_PRIVATE_KEY, BASE_PRIVATE_KEY, POLYGON_PRIVATE_KEY all used in execution-engine src/setupTests.ts and initialization code but absent from .env.example.
expected: Private key env vars documented in .env.example with NEVER_COMMIT warnings
actual: All chain private key vars absent from .env.example — operators have no documented path for key configuration.
recommendation: Add all *_PRIVATE_KEY vars to .env.example with NEVER_COMMIT warning and .env.local guidance.

title: OTEL observability vars not documented in .env.example
evidence: OTEL_EXPORTER_ENDPOINT, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME, OTEL_BATCH_SIZE, OTEL_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS used in shared/core/src/tracing/ and src/logging/otel-transport.ts but absent from .env.example. Operators enabling OpenTelemetry have no configuration guidance.
expected: OTEL_EXPORTER_ENDPOINT and related vars documented in .env.example
actual: All 4 OTEL_* vars absent — tracing feature is invisible to operators reading .env.example.
recommendation: Add OTEL_EXPORTER_ENDPOINT, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME, OTEL_BATCH_SIZE, OTEL_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS to .env.example under an Observability section.

title: Admin auth tokens DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN and CIRCUIT_BREAKER_API_KEY undocumented
evidence: DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN used in services/coordinator/src/api/routes/dashboard.routes.ts:53,65 — skips auth in production if absent, issues warning. CIRCUIT_BREAKER_API_KEY used in services/execution-engine/src/api/circuit-breaker-api.ts:105 for admin endpoint protection. Both absent from .env.example.
expected: Admin auth tokens documented in .env.example
actual: Both absent — production deployments may leave admin endpoints unauthenticated without operator awareness.
recommendation: Add DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN and CIRCUIT_BREAKER_API_KEY to .env.example with REQUIRED_IN_PRODUCTION notes.

title: 12 undocumented feature flag env vars in code
evidence: Feature flags in code but absent from .env.example: FEATURE_FAST_LANE, FEATURE_BACKRUN_STRATEGY, FEATURE_DEST_CHAIN_FLASH_LOAN, FEATURE_KMS_SIGNING, FEATURE_LIQUIDITY_DEPTH_SIZING, FEATURE_MEV_SHARE_BACKRUN, FEATURE_ML_SIGNAL_SCORING, FEATURE_MOMENTUM_TRACKING, FEATURE_ORDERFLOW_PIPELINE, FEATURE_SIGNAL_CACHE_READ, FEATURE_TIMEBOOST, FEATURE_UNISWAPX_FILLER.
expected: All FEATURE_* flags documented in .env.example
actual: 12 feature flag env vars absent — operators cannot discover available optional features.
recommendation: Add all FEATURE_* vars to .env.example under a Feature Flags section showing disabled-by-default values.

title: GAS_PRICE_MANTLE_GWEI and GAS_PRICE_MODE_GWEI missing from .env.example
evidence: GAS_PRICE_MANTLE_GWEI and GAS_PRICE_MODE_GWEI used in services/execution-engine/src/services/gas-price-optimizer.ts:198-199 but absent from .env.example. All other chain GAS_PRICE_*_GWEI vars are documented.
expected: Complete set of GAS_PRICE_*_GWEI vars documented in .env.example
actual: 2 of 15 chain gas price vars absent — inconsistent documentation for chain gas overrides.
recommendation: Add GAS_PRICE_MANTLE_GWEI and GAS_PRICE_MODE_GWEI to .env.example alongside existing GAS_PRICE_*_GWEI entries.

title: Trade logging vars (TRADE_LOG_ENABLED, TRADE_LOG_DIR) not in .env.example
evidence: TRADE_LOG_ENABLED and TRADE_LOG_DIR used in services/execution-engine/src/engine.ts:385-386,571. Trade logger is a critical operational component writing append-only JSONL files with daily rotation. Both vars absent from .env.example.
expected: Trade logging configuration vars documented in .env.example
actual: Both absent — operators unaware trade logging is enabled by default or how to configure the log directory.
recommendation: Add TRADE_LOG_ENABLED=true and TRADE_LOG_DIR=./data/trades to .env.example with operational notes.

title: 28 vars in .env.example have no active process.env usage
evidence: Orphaned: COMMIT_REVEAL_VALIDATE_PROFIT, CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, COW_BACKRUN_ETH_PRICE_USD, EXECUTION_TIMEOUT_MS, FAILOVER_THRESHOLD, FAILOVER_TIMEOUT_MS, HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS, JITO_TIP_LAMPORTS, LEADER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS, LEADER_LOCK_TTL_MS, MAX_GAS_PRICE_GWEI, MIN_PROFIT_PERCENTAGE, NONCE_POOL_REPLENISH_THRESHOLD, NONCE_POOL_SIZE, PROVIDER_CONNECTIVITY_TIMEOUT_MS, PROVIDER_HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS, PROVIDER_RECONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS, RPC_BATCH_MAX_QUEUE, RPC_BATCH_MAX_SIZE, RPC_BATCH_TIMEOUT_MS, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS (code uses SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS), SOLANA_MAX_PRICE_DEVIATION_PCT, SOLANA_MAX_SLIPPAGE_BPS, STAT_ARB_MAX_AGE_MS, STAT_ARB_MIN_CONFIDENCE, STAT_ARB_MIN_PROFIT_USD, TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_MS, UNIFIED_DETECTOR_PORT.
expected: All vars in .env.example correspond to active code usage
actual: 28 orphaned vars — likely removed features or renamed vars (e.g. SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS renamed to SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS).
recommendation: Audit each orphaned var: remove dead ones; fix renames (SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS -> SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS). JITO_TIP_LAMPORTS vs JITO_TIP_ACCOUNTS rename likely.

title: No nullish coalescing anti-patterns (|| 0 / || 0n) found
evidence: Searched services/ and shared/ source files (excluding __tests__ and node_modules) for || 0 and || 0n patterns. Zero matches found. Code correctly uses ?? 0 / ?? 0n throughout.
expected: No || 0 anti-patterns
actual: Confirmed clean.
recommendation: No action required. ESLint no-restricted-syntax rule is effective.
